resolution.notes: Distinct sum tasks: one filters positives while the other sums all values; keep both for data-processing variety.
Same even-check pattern but different prompt wording and implementation detail using explicit remainder; acceptable simple utility variety.
Loop skeleton is similar, but one sums values and the other counts positives; distinct behavior and prompt intent.
Both are summation examples, but one is an existing simple loop and the other is typed medium data-processing; keep as acceptable seed overlap.
Both are return-type mismatch examples, but use different literals and batches; keep as intentional error-class coverage.
Both cover return type mismatches in opposite directions/intended return types; distinct fixes and learning targets.
Both are missing semicolon after return, but use different functions and arithmetic contexts; keep as Jac semicolon coverage.
Both are semicolon syntax issues, one after return and one after assignment in entry; distinct statement contexts.
Both are type mismatch fixes but target different declared return types and corrected literals; keep distinct.
Both are undefined-name examples, but use different variable names and arithmetic functions; acceptable scoped-name coverage.
High normalized similarity from small snippets, but errors differ: undefined variable versus missing closing brace.
Both replace unresolved imports with local functions, but imported symbols and function bodies differ; keep as missing-module variants.
Different root causes: missing semicolon versus undefined parameter; keep as distinct debug tasks.
Different syntax errors in entry block: missing closing brace versus missing semicolon after print.
Both are typed variable initializer mismatches with different literals; keep for repeated primitive type-error coverage.
One is a typed node-field initializer error and the other a missing walker closing brace; distinct Jac constructs.
Both are archetype field type mismatches, but one node and one walker; keep to cover construct-specific field validation.
One missing walker brace and one walker field type mismatch; distinct walker error modes.
Different root causes: return type mismatch versus missing closing function brace.
Different syntax errors: missing semicolon after assignment versus missing closing parenthesis in function call.
Different root causes: entry assignment semicolon versus undefined argument name.
Different root causes in similar call snippets: missing parenthesis versus undefined variable.
Different debug scenarios: return type mismatch versus unresolved import replaced by local function.
Both are unresolved import examples, but symbols/domains differ; keep as acceptable import-error variants.
Both explain clamp functions with different sample values and granularity; keep as acceptable explanation overlap.
Both explain while loops but with opposite count directions and different line-by-line behavior.
Both explain stateful add methods, but one is a node Counter and one an obj Score with different fields/defaults.
Different control-flow explanations: falsy string fallback versus numeric max if/else.
Both process even numbers but outputs differ: list of evens versus sum of evens; keep distinct conversion targets.
